1	# eden-web-ui
     2	
     3	Reference Web UI service for the EDEN protocol. Phase 9 chunks 1
     4	(UI shell + planner module), 9c (implementer module), 9d
     5	(evaluator module), and 9e (admin / observability) ship — a human
     6	can play any of the three worker roles end-to-end through a
     7	browser, observe the experiment's task / trial / event state from
     8	`/admin/*`, and operator-reclaim a stranded claim. The implementer
     9	module and the work-ref GC sub-page of `/admin/*` are gated on the
    10	optional `--repo-path` flag; if omitted, the UI runs as a
    11	planner + evaluator + (read-only) admin deployment.
    12	
    13	The UI service is a **backend-for-frontend (BFF)**: it holds the
    14	`--shared-token` (the chapter 07 §12 reference bearer the
    15	orchestrator and worker hosts already use), runs `eden_wire.StoreClient`
    16	in-process to talk to the task-store-server, and exposes only
    17	server-rendered HTML to the browser. The browser never sees the
    18	shared token; it gets a signed session cookie.
    19	
    20	Rendering uses Jinja2 templates with [HTMX](https://htmx.org/) as a
    21	progressive-enhancement layer. Every mutating route works without
    22	JS (plain form-POST + 303-redirect / re-render); HTMX-aware routes
    23	additionally return a fragment when the browser sends
    24	`HX-Request: true`. The chunk-1 example is "add another proposal
    25	row" — HTMX appends one new row inline; without JS the same
    26	button does a full-page re-render. HTMX is vendored at
    27	`src/eden_web_ui/static/htmx-1.9.12.min.js`
    28	(SHA-256 `449317ade7881e949510db614991e195c3a099c4c791c24dacec55f9f4a2a452`)
    29	so the UI works offline and in CI without external network.
    30	
    31	## Run locally
    32	
    33	```bash
    34	python3 -m eden_task_store_server \
    35	    --db-path /tmp/eden.sqlite \
    36	    --experiment-id exp-1 \
    37	    --experiment-config tests/fixtures/experiment/.eden/config.yaml \
    38	    --port 0 \
    39	    --shared-token devtoken \
    40	    &
    41	
    42	# (Read EDEN_TASK_STORE_LISTENING from stdout to learn the port.)
    43	
    44	python3 -m eden_web_ui \
    45	    --task-store-url http://127.0.0.1:<port> \
    46	    --experiment-id exp-1 \
    47	    --experiment-config tests/fixtures/experiment/.eden/config.yaml \
    48	    --shared-token devtoken \
    49	    --session-secret "$(openssl rand -hex 32)" \
    50	    --artifacts-dir /tmp/eden-artifacts \
    51	    --port 0
    52	```
    53	
    54	The web-ui announces `EDEN_WEB_UI_LISTENING host=... port=...` on stdout
    55	on bind so harnesses (and the test suite) can discover the ephemeral
    56	port without scraping logs.
    57	
    58	## Auth model
    59	
    60	- The session cookie holds `{worker_id, csrf}` and is signed with
    61	  `--session-secret` via `itsdangerous`.
    62	- Cookie attributes: `HttpOnly`, `SameSite=Lax`, `Path=/`. `Secure`
    63	  is opt-in via `--secure-cookies` (use behind TLS).
    64	- Every mutating route validates a `csrf_token` form field in
    65	  constant time. The cookie's `SameSite=Lax` is **not** treated as
    66	  sufficient on its own.
    67	- The shared bearer never reaches the browser, the rendered HTML,
    68	  any session cookie, or any structured log line.
    69	
    70	## Stranded-claim recovery
    71	
    72	Every UI claim sets `expires_at = now + --claim-ttl-seconds`
    73	(default 1 hour). The orchestrator service runs
    74	`eden_dispatch.sweep_expired_claims` once per iteration so claims
    75	abandoned by closing the tab are reclaimed automatically — no
    76	operator action required.
    77	
    78	## Planner submit flow
    79	
    80	The planner module pins three phases:
    81	
    82	1. **Phase 1 — drafting.** For every proposal: write rationale
    83	   markdown to `<artifacts-dir>/<proposal_id>.md` (atomically, via
    84	   tmp-and-rename), build a `file://` URI, then call
    85	   `store.create_proposal(state="drafting")`. Drafting proposals
    86	   are invisible to the orchestrator's dispatch path.
    87	2. **Phase 2 — ready.** Loop over the just-created proposals and
    88	   call `store.mark_proposal_ready` for each.
    89	3. **Phase 3 — submit.** `store.submit(...)` with retry-before-orphan
    90	   on transport-shaped failures (3 attempts, exponential backoff,
    91	   leveraging chapter 07 §2.4 / §8.1 idempotent resubmit). On a
    92	   definitive divergent response or after the retries are
    93	   exhausted, the orphaned-proposals error page lists the
    94	   `ready`-but-unreferenced proposal IDs for operator recovery.
    95	
    96	The narrowest unsafe window is between Phase 2 and Phase 3:
    97	proposals are `ready` but not yet referenced by a submitted plan
    98	task. We accept this for the reference impl; it applies equally
    99	to the existing scripted planner host. A spec-level fix (atomic
   100	ready-and-submit) is out of scope for chunk 1.
   101	
   102	## Implementer module (chunk 9c)
   103	
   104	The implementer module is registered when `--repo-path <path>` is
   105	set on the CLI; it points at the same bare git repo the
   106	`eden_implementer_host` service writes `work/*` refs into. The
   107	top-level navigation hides the "implementer" link otherwise and
   108	the routes return 404.
   109	
   110	Trust model and assumption: the **user does git work in their own
   111	checkout out-of-band**, then pushes their tip commit to the bare
   112	repo (any branch — the UI creates the canonical `work/<slug>-<trial_id>`
   113	ref pointing at the commit when the form is submitted). The UI
   114	never accepts credentials, never runs `git push`, and never
   115	proxies a remote. Multi-machine deployments where the user's
   116	checkout and the bare repo are not on the same filesystem are out
   117	of scope until Phase 10's Compose stack and a later remote-repo
   118	story.
   119	
   120	**Spec-to-code map for `POST /implementer/{task_id}/submit`:**
   121	
   122	1. **Validate** the form: `status ∈ {success, error}`, `commit_sha`
   123	   is 40 lowercase hex when `status=success`.
   124	2. **§3.3 reachability check** (status=success only):
   125	   - `repo.commit_exists(commit_sha)` — rejects refs that were not
   126	     pushed to the bare repo with a clear "did you push it?" error.
   127	   - For every parent in `proposal.parent_commits`,
   128	     `repo.is_ancestor(parent, commit_sha)` — rejects commits whose
   129	     history does not descend from the proposal's declared parents
   130	     (per `spec/v0/03-roles.md` §3.3).
   131	3. **Pre-Phase-1 ref-collision guard** (status=success only):
   132	   `repo.ref_exists("refs/heads/work/<slug>-<trial_id>")` short-
   133	   circuits with a form re-render and no store mutation. Branch
   134	   uniqueness is required by §3.3 ("worker branch MUST be unique
   135	   to this trial"); the guard turns a vanishing edge case into a
   136	   clean form error.
   137	4. **Phase 1 — `store.create_trial`** with `status="starting"`,
   138	   no `commit_sha`. The orchestrator's `accept` handler is what
   139	   writes `commit_sha` onto the trial later, per
   140	   `eden_storage._base._accept_implement`. This ordering honors
   141	   `03-roles.md` §3.2 step 1 ("trial persisted before observable
   142	   repo writes").
   143	5. **Phase 2 — `repo.create_ref`** (status=success only): writes
   144	   `refs/heads/work/<slug>-<trial_id>` pointing at the user's
   145	   `commit_sha`. On status=error this step is skipped (no work
   146	   branch exists).
   147	6. **Phase 3 — `store.submit`** with retry-before-orphan plus a
   148	   committed-state read-back. The retry policy is identical to the
   149	   planner's: 3 attempts, backoff `(0.05, 0.2, 0.5)`, definitive
   150	   store-domain errors short-circuit. After retries are exhausted
   151	   on transport-shape failures, the route does
   152	   `read_task` + `read_submission` + `submissions_equivalent` to
   153	   distinguish "the prior attempt actually committed and the
   154	   response was lost" (renders the success page) from "no submit
   155	   committed" (renders the orphan page).
   156	
   157	**Per-error recovery summary** (renders prose on the orphan page):
   158	
   159	- *Phase 2 failure* (`create_ref` raises): trial sits in
   160	  `starting`, no `work/*` ref. Recovery: claim TTL → sweeper →
   161	  `reclaim` composite-commits the orphaned trial to `error`.
   162	- *Phase 3 retry exhaustion, server committed*: success page (the
   163	  read-back found our equivalent submission already on file).
   164	- *Phase 3 retry exhaustion, server never committed*: orphan
   165	  page; "auto-recovers via reclaim" prose.
   166	- *Phase 3 `WrongToken` / `IllegalTransition`*: the prior reclaim
   167	  that invalidated our token already errored our `starting`
   168	  trial; orphan page "auto-recovers via reclaim".
   169	- *Phase 3 `ConflictingResubmission`*: a different submission won
   170	  the race. Orphan page surfaces `trial_id` / `commit_sha` for
   171	  operator triage.
   172	
   173	**Artifact-rendering trust boundary.** The draft form may inline a
   174	proposal's rationale markdown if (a) `proposal.artifacts_uri`
   175	starts with `file://`, (b) the resolved path is contained within
   176	the UI service's `--artifacts-dir`, (c) the file is not larger
   177	than 1 MiB. Any other shape renders as a link only. This guards
   178	against a malicious / careless `artifacts_uri` pointing the UI at
   179	arbitrary local files.
   180	
   181	**`trial_id` is server-only.** Generated at claim time and stored
   182	alongside the claim token in the in-process `_CLAIMS` dict; never
   183	appears in the request surface (no hidden form field, no URL
   184	parameter). A forged `trial_id` form value is ignored.
   185	
   186	## Evaluator module (chunk 9d)
   187	
   188	The evaluator module mounts unconditionally — the evaluator never
   189	touches a repo through the UI (per `spec/v0/03-roles.md` §4.3 it
   190	reads the trial at `commit_sha` out-of-band and submits metrics
   191	back). The top-level navigation always shows the "evaluator" link.
   192	
   193	The draft page surfaces:
   194	
   195	- **Trial fields** (read-only): trial_id, branch, commit_sha,
   196	  parent_commits, status, started_at. `commit_sha` is what the
   197	  operator checks out from the bare repo to evaluate.
   198	- **Trial-side optional fields set by the implementer per §3.2
   199	  step 3**: `trial.description` (rendered as a read-only `<pre>`
   200	  block, escaped via Jinja2 autoescape) and `trial.artifacts_uri`
   201	  (rendered with the chunk-9c scheme allowlist + the same trust-
   202	  boundary helper as the proposal rationale).
   203	- **Proposal context** (slug, priority, artifacts_uri, rationale).
   204	- **Metrics form**: one input per metric in
   205	  `experiment_config.metrics_schema`, typed by the declared
   206	  `MetricType` (`<input type="number" step="1">` for `integer`,
   207	  `step="any"` for `real`, plain text otherwise).
   208	- **Submission status**: radio for `success` / `error` /
   209	  `eval_error` per §4.4.
   210	- **Optional `artifacts_uri`** text input (the operator's URI for
   211	  their eval logs / outputs, uploaded out-of-band).
   212	
   213	**Spec-to-code map for `POST /evaluator/{task_id}/submit`:**
   214	
   215	1. **Validate** the form. `parse_evaluate_form` returns `(None,
   216	   errors)` on:
   217	   - `status` outside `{"success", "error", "eval_error"}`.
   218	   - Per-metric type drift (integer accepts `1.0` per
   219	     `02-data-model.md` §1.3 but rejects `1.5`; real rejects
   220	     `nan`/`±inf`; text must be non-empty after strip).

1	# EDEN Roadmap
     2	
     3	Incremental build-up from empty repo to a complete EDEN protocol specification, reference implementation, and conformance suite.
     4	
     5	## Two orthogonal concepts
     6	
     7	- **Units** — the fine-grained decomposition. Each unit is a distinct, named piece of work with its own exit criterion. Units are the progress-tracking granularity: a unit can be crossed off even when it ships in a shared commit with siblings.
     8	- **Chunks** — execution grouping. Units that must be designed and implemented together (cross-referencing spec chapters, protocol alongside its first consumer, shared commit-sized work) travel as one chunk. Chunks are the review/commit granularity.
     9	
    10	**Rule:** a chunk can span multiple units; no unit spans chunks. If implementation runs over context mid-chunk, stop at the next unit boundary — never mid-unit. That keeps handoffs, resumes, and partial progress legible across sessions.
    11	
    12	Each phase also has explicit **non-goals** to prevent creep.
    13	
    14	---
    15	
    16	## Phase 0 — Bootstrap
    17	
    18	Repo shape, documentation shell, CI, GitHub hygiene. No runnable code and no spec content.
    19	
    20	**Units:**
    21	
    22	- **0a** — Repo scaffold (directory tree + section READMEs + root docs + CI workflow + `.gitignore` + `.markdownlint.json` + `CLAUDE.md` symlink). First `main` commit on GitHub; branch protection requiring `docs-lint`.
    23	
    24	**Chunk:** 0a is one chunk — this bootstrap.
    25	
    26	**Non-goals:** any spec chapter content; any JSON Schema files; any Python; any reference code; any conformance scenarios.
    27	
    28	**Exit:** `docs-lint` CI job green on `main`; protected branch in place; all root and section READMEs accurately describe what's in the repo and what's next.
    29	
    30	---
    31	
    32	## Phase 1 — Spec v0 core concepts + schemas + fixture migration
    33	
    34	Writes the first normative chapters and the JSON Schemas they describe.
    35	
    36	**Units:**
    37	
    38	- **1a** — `spec/v0/00-overview.md`, `01-concepts.md`, `02-data-model.md` prose. Defines experiment, trial, proposal, role, artifact, metric, worker.
    39	- **1b** — JSON Schemas for config, task, event, proposal, trial, metrics-meta under `spec/v0/schemas/`. Each cited from the data-model chapter.
    40	- **1c** — Migrate `tests/fixtures/experiment/.eden/config.yaml` from direvo; assert `experiment-config.schema.json` validates it. Add a `schema-validity` CI job.
    41	
    42	**Chunk:** 1a + 1b + 1c — one chunk. Prose, schemas, and fixture cross-constrain each other; splitting would produce inconsistency.
    43	
    44	**Non-goals:** role contracts (Phase 2); event/integrator/storage chapters (Phase 4); any Pydantic code (Phase 3).
    45	
    46	**Exit:** schemas validate the fixture; `schema-validity` CI green; `02-data-model.md` cross-references all six schemas.
    47	
    48	---
    49	
    50	## Phase 2 — Spec v0 role contracts + task protocol
    51	
    52	Two more chapters. Defines what each role does and the task lifecycle they operate against.
    53	
    54	**Units:**
    55	
    56	- **2a** — `03-roles.md`. Planner, implementer, evaluator contracts: discovery, claiming, context reading, execution, submission, release. Per-role outputs and their expected schema shapes.
    57	- **2b** — `04-task-protocol.md`. Full task state machine (legal and illegal transitions enumerated), claim-token semantics, submit idempotency rule, wire format for task objects.
    58	
    59	**Chunk:** 2a + 2b — one chunk. Role contracts reference task lifecycle and vice versa.
    60	
    61	**Non-goals:** event protocol (Phase 4); any implementation (Phase 5+).
    62	
    63	**Exit:** state machine is pinned; `04-task-protocol.md` enumerates every legal transition with its pre- and post-conditions.
    64	
    65	---
    66	
    67	## Phase 3 — Reference contracts package (`eden-contracts`)
    68	
    69	First real Python code: Pydantic bindings for the v0 JSON Schemas. Introduces the uv workspace + Python toolchain.
    70	
    71	**Units:**
    72	
    73	- **3a** — Pydantic models for the six v0 schemas, along with the root `pyproject.toml` (uv workspace root), a per-package `reference/packages/eden-contracts/pyproject.toml`, and a `.python-version` file. Ruff/pyright config ported from direvo. CI jobs: `python-lint` (ruff), `python-typecheck` (pyright), `python-test` (pytest).
    74	- **3b** — Schema ↔ model parity check. CI job `schema-parity` that loads each JSON Schema and asserts the matching Pydantic model can round-trip any instance; fails CI on drift.
    75	
    76	**Chunk:** 3a + 3b — one chunk.
    77	
    78	**Non-goals:** any domain logic, just data types; storage, git, dispatch (later phases).
    79	
    80	**Exit:** all six Pydantic models exist and parity CI is green against the Phase 1 schemas.
    81	
    82	---
    83	
    84	## Phase 4 — Spec v0 events + integrator + storage
    85	
    86	Three more chapters. Heavy cross-referencing: the integrator emits events; storage persists them.
    87	
    88	**Units:**
    89	
    90	- **4a** — `05-event-protocol.md` + `event.schema.json` refinement. Transactional invariant (state change + event insert must commit atomically); delivery guarantees subscribers can rely on.
    91	- **4b** — `06-integrator.md`. Git topology invariants: `work/*`, `trial/*`, `main` namespaces; sole-integrator rule; squash rule; eval-manifest shape (metrics + blob URIs + hashes).
    92	- **4c** — `08-storage.md`. Repository interface as protocol-level contract; durability requirements; per-experiment metrics schemas.
    93	
    94	**Chunk:** 4a + 4b + 4c — one chunk. Cross-references are dense and splitting risks inconsistency.
    95	
    96	**Non-goals:** control-plane (Phase 12); conformance chapter (Phase 11); any code.
    97	
    98	**Exit:** three chapters land with consistent cross-references; schema updates (if any) pass parity.
    99	
   100	---
   101	
   102	## Phase 5 — In-memory reference dispatch loop
   103	
   104	First executable implementation. Proves the spec's state machines are implementable. Single process; no git yet; no persistence.
   105	
   106	**Units:**
   107	
   108	- **5a — complete.** `eden_dispatch.InMemoryStore` collapses the task store, event log, and proposal/trial persistence into one in-process object. Every mutating operation stages writes in a `_Tx` and applies them in a single `_commit`; precondition failures raise before commit, so readers never observe a partial state change. All six composite-commit patterns from `05-event-protocol.md` §2.2 land atomically.
   109	- **5b — complete.** `ScriptedPlanner`, `ScriptedImplementer`, and `ScriptedEvaluator` participate in the real task protocol (claim → execute → submit) with deterministic fake outputs. The Phase 5 in-process `run_experiment` driver orchestrated a full plan → implement → evaluate → integrate cycle in a single process; it was deleted in Phase 8c, with the same flow now exercised end-to-end by the real-subprocess `pytest.mark.e2e` test.
   110	- **5c — complete.** 36 pytest scenarios cover every legal transition in §1.2, every negative rule, claim-token authorization (§3.3), idempotent resubmit (§4.2), terminal immutability (§4.4), the reclamation policy (§5), every composite commit (§2.2), and a 3-trial end-to-end experiment whose event log alone reconstructs every entity's lifecycle.
   111	
   112	**Chunks:** 5a one chunk; 5b + 5c one chunk (scenarios need a worker harness to drive).
   113	
   114	**Non-goals:** git integration; SQLite persistence; cross-process.
   115	
   116	**Exit achieved:** the in-memory loop runs a 3-trial experiment end to end; 36 conformance scenarios pass; all CI gates green.
   117	
   118	---
   119	
   120	## Phase 6 — Reference storage backend (`eden-storage`) — complete
   121	
   122	Makes Phase 5 persist across restarts.
   123

     5	## What this repo is
     6	
     7	EDEN is a **protocol** for directed-code-evolution orchestration, not a single product. The repo contains three distinct kinds of artifact, each with different change discipline:
     8	
     9	| Layer | Path | Authoritative? | Change discipline |
    10	|---|---|---|---|
    11	| Protocol specification | [`spec/`](spec/) | Yes — the source of truth | RFC-style: versioned, normative (MUST/SHOULD/MAY), carefully reviewed |
    12	| Reference implementation | [`reference/`](reference/) | No — *one* valid impl | Normal code-review discipline |
    13	| Conformance suite | [`conformance/`](conformance/) | Normative for tests | Black-box; must be implementation-agnostic |
    14	
    15	Non-normative human docs live in [`docs/`](docs/).

    27	**Phase 8c complete.** The in-process dispatch driver is removed: `eden-dispatch.run_experiment` was deleted and its public-API surface narrows to `run_orchestrator_iteration` plus the scripted workers (`ScriptedPlanner` / `ScriptedImplementer` / `ScriptedEvaluator`). The Phase 5 / 7b in-process end-to-end tests in `reference/packages/eden-dispatch/tests/test_end_to_end.py` were deleted; the lifecycle-reconstruction invariant they covered moved into `reference/services/orchestrator/tests/test_e2e.py` as a `_reconstruct_lifecycle` fold over the post-teardown `SqliteStore.read_range()`. The malformed-success → `validation_error` routing test in `eden-storage/tests/test_store_hardening.py` was rewritten to drive `run_orchestrator_iteration` directly through the legitimate `Store` API. The `pytest.mark.e2e` real-subprocess test from Phase 8b is now the only end-to-end coverage path; components communicate exclusively through the Phase 8a wire binding.
    28	
    29	**Phase 8b complete.** Five reference services under [`reference/services/`](reference/services/) extract the dispatch loop into independent OS processes that communicate only over the Phase 8a wire binding: [`task-store-server/`](reference/services/task-store-server/) hosts the `Store` behind uvicorn; [`orchestrator/`](reference/services/orchestrator/) runs the finalize/dispatch/integrate half of the loop against a `StoreClient`; [`planner/`](reference/services/planner/), [`implementer/`](reference/services/implementer/), and [`evaluator/`](reference/services/evaluator/) are standalone worker hosts. A new shared package [`reference/services/_common/`](reference/services/_common/) holds the JSON-line logger, SIGTERM/SIGINT stop flag, readiness probe, common argparse flags, scripted `plan_fn`/`implement_fn`/`evaluate_fn` profiles, and the `seed_bare_repo` helper. `eden-dispatch` exposes `run_orchestrator_iteration` as its orchestrator-side entry point so the standalone orchestrator drives only that half (the in-process `run_experiment` driver remained alongside it through 8b and was deleted in 8c). `eden-wire.make_app(store, *, shared_token=…)` and `eden-wire.StoreClient(…, token=…)` add reference-only bearer-token auth: requests without the right `Authorization: Bearer <token>` are rejected as `eden://reference-error/unauthorized` (HTTP 401), under a new namespace deliberately kept out of the normative `eden://error/…` vocabulary in [`spec/v0/07-wire-protocol.md`](spec/v0/07-wire-protocol.md) §7. A new informative §12 in chapter 07 documents the scheme. The implementer host writes real git commits (single-parent or merge, honoring `proposal.parent_commits`) on `work/*` branches in the bare repo the orchestrator integrates from. A `pytest.mark.e2e` test in [`reference/services/orchestrator/tests/test_e2e.py`](reference/services/orchestrator/tests/test_e2e.py) forks all five processes on an ephemeral port, drives a 3-trial experiment to quiescence over real HTTP + real SQLite + real git, and tears down with SIGTERM → SIGKILL fallback.
    30	
    31	**Phase 8a complete.** `eden-wire` ships an HTTP binding for the chapter 4 / 5 / 6 §3.4 / 8 §§1.1–2.1 operations specified in the new [`spec/v0/07-wire-protocol.md`](spec/v0/07-wire-protocol.md) chapter. The package exposes a FastAPI ``make_app(store)`` that routes every wire endpoint to a ``Store`` instance, and a ``StoreClient`` that satisfies the same ``Store`` Protocol against the HTTP surface — so existing callers (dispatch driver, integrator) work across the process boundary unchanged. Errors round-trip as RFC 7807 problem+json with a closed vocabulary of ``eden://error/<name>`` types (§7). ``Store.integrate_trial`` is now same-value idempotent (§5 of the chapter); the ``Integrator`` distinguishes different-SHA divergence (``AtomicityViolation``, no ref compensation) from other synchronous rejections (normal §3.4 compensating-delete flow). ``StoreClient.integrate_trial`` reconciles transport-indeterminate failures via read-back: the three outcomes are confirmed success, confirmed divergence (``InvalidPrecondition``), or ``IndeterminateIntegration`` when the server's outcome cannot be determined. Polling + long-poll-style subscribe are both bound (§6). Worker-process extraction, SSE/WebSocket push, and cut-over of in-process paths remain Phase 8b / 8c.
    32	
    33	**Phase 6 complete.** The `eden-storage` package ships the `Store` structural interface for the task store, event log, and proposal/trial persistence sides of chapter 8 — collapsed into a single Protocol per §7 implementation latitude. Two conforming backends satisfy it: `InMemoryStore` (fast, non-durable; moved from `eden-dispatch`) and `SqliteStore` (durable across restarts via a WAL-mode SQLite database with `synchronous=FULL`, matching §3.1's crash-survival requirement). The Protocol covers the spec-literal `create_task` / `replay` / `read_range` operations alongside the typed convenience helpers. Both backends share the transition logic in `_base.py`, and the same conformance scenarios are parametrized across both — drift from the Protocol surfaces in tests, not in production. Restart-safety tests close and reopen a SQLite store mid-experiment to confirm state, event log, and claim tokens survive, and a monkey-patched `_apply_commit` failure verifies rollback. The artifact store (§5), `subscribe` streaming (§2.1), and Postgres remain non-goals for Phase 6; they land in Phase 10 / Phase 8 / later respectively.
    34	
    35	**Phase 7b complete.** `eden-git` now also ships the `Integrator` that composes `GitRepo` with a `Store` to promote `success` trials per chapter 6. Given a trial with a recorded `commit_sha`, `Integrator.integrate` builds the §3.2 single-commit squash (worker-tip tree plus the eval manifest at `.eden/trials/<trial_id>/eval.json`), writes the `refs/heads/trial/<id>-<slug>` ref via zero-oid CAS, and routes the store's atomic `integrate_trial` write for `trial_commit_sha` and the `trial.integrated` event. On store failure the ref is compensatingly deleted per §3.4, matching the post-promotion reading recorded in [`spec/v0/design-notes/integrator-atomicity.md`](spec/v0/design-notes/integrator-atomicity.md). Re-invocation on an already-promoted trial is a verified no-op (§5.3): ref SHA, squash tree shape, and manifest bytes are re-derived and compared. §2 preconditions (`status == success`, `commit_sha` reachable from `branch` tip), §1.4 reachability, and §2 metrics validity are all enforced up front; the new public `Store.validate_metrics` closes the §2 MUST-NOT-promote clause even if upstream orchestrator validation were bypassed. The spec itself was tightened at §3.4 to make the post-promotion reading explicit. The dispatch driver accepted `integrate_trial: Callable[[str], object]` in place of the Phase 5 placeholder `integrator_commit_factory` parameter; in Phase 8c that hook moved to `run_orchestrator_iteration`. Eval-manifest bytes are deterministic (sorted keys, `indent=2`, trailing newline) to make §5.3 idempotency re-derivation stable.

    37	**Phase 7a complete.** `eden-git`'s subprocess wrapper ships `GitRepo` covering ref/object inspection (`rev_parse`, `resolve_ref`, `list_refs`, `is_ancestor`, `ls_tree`), plumbing (`write_blob`, `write_tree_from_entries`, `write_tree_with_file`, `commit_tree`, `create_ref`, `update_ref`), worktree management, and branch management. Author identity and `commit.gpgsign=false` are pinned per-invocation so the user's ambient git config never leaks into integrator commits. See [`docs/roadmap.md`](docs/roadmap.md) for the full 13-phase plan.

    66	## Contribution conventions
    67	
    68	### Spec edits
    69	
    70	+ Every cross-component contract (wire format, state machine, invariant) is defined in `spec/` first, then implemented in `reference/`. When they disagree, the spec wins and the impl gets a bug.
    71	+ Spec chapters use RFC 2119 language: MUST, SHOULD, MAY.
    72	+ Spec versions are frozen once stable (`spec/v0/` is a single lineage; breaking changes go to `spec/v1/`).
    73	+ Schema changes must update the spec prose, the JSON Schema file, and (from Phase 3 onward) the Pydantic bindings in lockstep. CI will enforce schema ↔ model parity once both exist.
    74	
    75	### Reference implementation
    76	
    77	+ Normal code-review discipline.
    78	+ Normative behavior is tested against the conformance suite (once it exists in Phase 11).
    79	+ The reference impl is explicitly not a monopoly on correctness. A third-party implementation that passes conformance is equally valid.
    80	
    81	### Adding a new service or package with its own `tests/` directory
    82	
    83	Pytest's default `prepend` import mode names test modules by file basename, so two `tests/test_app.py` files in different packages collide on collection (`import file mismatch`). The repo's tests-dir layout is mixed — some packages (e.g. [`eden-contracts/tests/`](reference/packages/eden-contracts/tests/)) have `__init__.py` and use `from .conftest import …`; most service test dirs (e.g. [`web-ui/tests/`](reference/services/web-ui/tests/)) have no `__init__.py` and use `from conftest import …`.
    84	
    85	When adding a new tests directory:
    86	
    87	+ Use a basename that is unique across the whole `testpaths` set in [`pyproject.toml`](pyproject.toml). Grep `find reference -name 'test_*.py' -exec basename {} \;` to verify before merging.
    88	+ Do **not** add `__init__.py` to a service tests dir while another tests dir already has one — both directories become the `tests` package and the second conftest collides on `tests.conftest` import/registration. Under default `prepend` mode this surfaces as `ImportPathMismatchError`; under `importlib` mode it surfaces as `Plugin already registered under a different name`.
    89	+ Switching the whole repo to `--import-mode=importlib` looks tempting but is **not** a clean fix today: the existing eden-contracts tests use relative `.conftest` imports that depend on the `__init__.py` package layout, and importlib mode keeps that collision while removing the sys.path hack the rest of the suite relies on.
    90	
    91	If the constraint becomes load-bearing later (Phase 10 will add several more service test dirs), the cleanest fix is a three-step lockstep: rename each tests dir to a unique package name (e.g. `web_ui_tests/` instead of `tests/`), switch every bare `from conftest import …` to a package-relative `from .conftest import …`, and add `--import-mode=importlib` to pytest's `addopts`. A mechanical basename-uniqueness check in CI is also a sensible follow-up — AGENTS-only guidance is weaker than a guardrail.
    92	
    93	### Adding or extending a JSON Schema + Pydantic binding
    94	
    95	The `schema-parity` CI job is only as strong as what both sides of the test actually enforce. Several Pydantic and `jsonschema` defaults let drift through silently. When adding a new schema — or a new field type to an existing one — evaluate each of the following; reusable implementations live in [`reference/packages/eden-contracts/src/eden_contracts/_common.py`](reference/packages/eden-contracts/src/eden_contracts/_common.py).
    96	
    97	+ **Strict numeric parsing.** Top-level models set `ConfigDict(strict=True, extra="allow")`. Non-strict mode coerces `True`/`"2"` into int, but the schemas treat `type: integer` / `type: number` as strict JSON types. New models must keep `strict=True`.
    98	+ **Format assertions on both sides.** `format` keywords (`uri`, `date-time`, …) are advisory by default in both the `jsonschema` library and Pydantic. The schema-side validator wires a custom `FormatChecker` in [`tests/conftest.py`](reference/packages/eden-contracts/tests/conftest.py), and the model side uses the reusable types in `_common.py`. A new `format` keyword in any schema requires handlers on *both* sides; the `test_format_coverage` test fails loudly if the schema-side handler is missing.
    99	+ **Real date-time / URI validation, not just regex or `urlparse`.** The regex on `DateTimeStr` accepts impossible values like `2026-99-99T…Z`; an `AfterValidator` runs `datetime.fromisoformat` to reject those. `UriStr` uses `rfc3986-validator`, not `urllib.parse.urlparse`, which accepts malformed schemeful URIs (e.g., spaces in the host).
   100	+ **Null vs absent.** JSON Schema's `type: X` rejects explicit `null`, but Pydantic's `X | None = None` accepts it. Wrap every optional typed field with `NotNone` from `_common.py` so absent is accepted and explicit null is rejected.
   101	+ **Round-trip emission.** `model.model_dump(mode="json", exclude_none=True)` must re-validate against the schema. Add any new model to `tests/test_roundtrip.py` so this is checked.
   102	+ **Corpus coverage.** Parity is asserted over the fixture corpus in `tests/cases.py`. A new field type deserves at least one accept fixture and one reject fixture per constraint the schema imposes (required, pattern, enum, min/max, format, cross-field).
   103	
   104	## Commit guidelines
   105	
   106	+ Short imperative subjects (e.g., "Add event protocol chapter", "Pin task state machine").
   107	+ For pull requests, include: what the change is, which phase it advances, and any spec ↔ impl implications.

   137	        # Mutate the trial back to starting between GET and POST. The

    71	) -> str | None:
    72	    """Return the artifact text iff ``uri`` resolves inside ``artifacts_dir``.
    73	
    74	    Trust-boundary helper used by both the proposal rationale
    75	    rendering (chunk 9c §A.1) and the trial-side artifact rendering
    76	    (chunk 9d §A.1):

    80	    - The resolved path MUST be contained within
    81	      ``artifacts_dir.resolve()``. ``..``-traversal and absolute
    82	      escapes are rejected via ``Path.is_relative_to``.
    83	    - Non-file inodes (directories, sockets) return ``None``.

    88	    parsed = urlparse(uri)

    91	    raw_path = unquote(parsed.path)

item.text:
**Findings**
1. Bug — [reference/services/web-ui/src/eden_web_ui/routes/admin.py](/Users/ericalt/Documents/eden/reference/services/web-ui/src/eden_web_ui/routes/admin.py:125) at lines 125, 185, 226, 298, 346, 421, and 470. The admin `GET` handlers call `store.*`/`repo.*` directly with no `DispatchError` or transport-exception handling, so a transient `StoreClient` failure becomes a 500. That conflicts with plan §G, which explicitly requires inline read-view placeholders for transport failures. Fix by mirroring the planner/evaluator pattern: catch store-domain errors and generic transport exceptions around each read path and render a banner/placeholder response instead of crashing.

2. Bug — [reference/services/web-ui/src/eden_web_ui/routes/admin.py](/Users/ericalt/Documents/eden/reference/services/web-ui/src/eden_web_ui/routes/admin.py:543). `work_refs_delete()` turns every exception from `repo.delete_ref()` into `?error=ref-changed`. Per plan §A.7/§G, only a real CAS miss should become `ref-changed`; other `GitError`s should surface as a 5xx (or a distinct transport error if you intentionally change the UX). As written, a permissions problem, broken repo, or other git failure is misreported as a benign race. Fix by catching `GitError` specifically, classifying the expected-old-SHA mismatch case, and re-raising unexpected git failures.

3. Risk — [reference/services/web-ui/src/eden_web_ui/routes/admin.py](/Users/ericalt/Documents/eden/reference/services/web-ui/src/eden_web_ui/routes/admin.py:70) and [reference/services/web-ui/tests/test_admin_routes.py](/Users/ericalt/Documents/eden/reference/services/web-ui/tests/test_admin_routes.py:113). `_coerce_filter()` maps unknown `kind`/`state` values to `None`, so `/admin/tasks/?kind=garbage` shows the full task list. Plan §A.3 says unknown values should yield an empty result, not silently disable filtering. The unit test currently locks in the opposite behavior. Fix by distinguishing `*`/absent from invalid values and returning an empty rowset for invalid filters; update the test accordingly.

4. Risk — [reference/services/web-ui/src/eden_web_ui/routes/admin.py](/Users/ericalt/Documents/eden/reference/services/web-ui/src/eden_web_ui/routes/admin.py:153) and [reference/services/web-ui/src/eden_web_ui/templates/admin_index.html](/Users/ericalt/Documents/eden/reference/services/web-ui/src/eden_web_ui/templates/admin_index.html:69). The dashboard swallows `repo.list_refs()` failures and reuses `work_ref_count is None` for both “`--repo-path` not configured” and “git failed”. That violates plan §G, which says git read failures should surface as errors, and it misleads the operator into thinking GC is simply disabled. Fix by reserving `None` for `repo is None` and using a separate error state, or let the git failure propagate to the standard error page.

**Overall assessment**

The happy-path admin flows are well-covered and the core integration looks solid; the targeted admin test suite and the admin e2e test both pass. The main gaps are around failure-mode fidelity and plan adherence: read-path transport handling is missing, git delete failures are over-collapsed, and the task-filter semantics drifted from the design and were codified in tests.
